title: Paper 4 — Typed Boundary Repair: A Unified Failure-Preservation Transport Operator
abstract: A typed boundary repair converts a failed join between two charts on a shared boundary into a typed proof-obligation row that preserves the D4 axis class and the sheet value of the boundary. The repair operation is idempotent on already-normalized rows. Untyped failures cannot be consumed as repair evidence. The Arf-matching criterion (Paper 3) is the consistency condition. The D4 axis/sheet matching is the structural condition. The repair operation produces a row in the obligation ledger with explicit lane, source, and resolution. This is the CQECMPLX failure-preservation transport law.

D_claims.claim: D4 axis/sheet codec: 4 axis classes × 2 sheets = 8 LCR states
Arf invariant of correction quadratic form Q = C + CR is 0
Arf-matching edge glue: two charts can be joined iff Arf invariants match on shared boundary
OBLIGATION_ROWS.json contains 1,105 rows, 9 receipt_bound, 5 hold papers
8 claim lanes exist with explicit schema
7 evidence classes exist with explicit schema
Binary boundary adapter: adapt, anneal_to_lie_conjugate, matching_tail, CIRCLE_F, CIRCLE_P, CORRECTION_FIRING
Transport obligations: 4 layers with explicit classifications and witnesses
Concrete correction states (0,1,0) and (1,1,0) with Paper 3 coordinates (2,0) and (3,1)

I_claims.claim: Typed boundary repair as a specific operation with 4 components (lane, source, resolution, falsifier)
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading; no typed_boundary_repair.py module exists in lattice_forge/

I_claims.claim: Type preservation theorem (repair preserves D4 axis class and sheet)
I_claims.reasoning: Framing; the math is that repair preserves chart value, which is a logical consequence of Arf-matching

I_claims.claim: Idempotence theorem (repair applied twice = same ledger row)
I_claims.reasoning: Framing; idempotence is natural but not literally in the data

I_claims.claim: Exclusion of untyped failures (minimal falsifier {status:failed} rejected)
I_claims.reasoning: Standard typed-evidence doctrine applied as structural framing

I_claims.claim: Ledger row as 5-tuple (id, ℓ, s, e, τ)
I_claims.reasoning: Framing; OBLIGATION_ROWS.json has a different but consistent schema
Structural reading of OBLIGATION_ROWS.json status field (actual statuses are 5 variants)

I_claims.claim: Failure-preservation transport law and constraint-transport physics map
I_claims.reasoning: Author's structural reading of the CQECMPLX transport discipline

I_claims.claim: Civil-engineering crack map as non-toy domain analogy
I_claims.reasoning: Analog interpretation; faithful mapping but not derived from code

I_claims.claim: Repair is a constraint, not a proof — this is its correct transport type
I_claims.reasoning: Structural reading of the CQECMPLX honesty discipline
